name: NIMCP_Brain_Immune_System
observations: Biologically-inspired immune coordination layer (B cells, T cells, antibodies, cytokines)
Added bio-async integration to 24+ immune bridge modules (Dec 2024)
Added BIO_MODULE_* definitions for all immune bridges (0x0D00 - 0x0DFF range)
Fixed logging macros from nimcp_log to NIMCP_LOGGING_* and LOG_MODULE_*
Fixed time function calls to nimcp_time_get_ms/us
All 47 brain immune unit tests passing
Integration with BBB, BFT, swarm immune subsystems

name: NIMCP_Bio_Async_Integration
observations: Bio-async router provides inter-module messaging
Each immune bridge registers with bio_router_register_module()
Uses BIO_MODULE_* identifiers from nimcp_bio_messages.h
Standard API: connect_bio_async, disconnect_bio_async, is_bio_async_connected
Struct fields: bio_module_context_t bio_ctx, bool bio_async_enabled

name: NIMCP_Phase_1_1_Medulla
observations: Implemented Dec 2025
5 modules: arousal_state, protective_cutoff, brainstem_coupling, circadian, medulla orchestrator
~5981 lines of production code
Bio-async integration for all modules
Models brainstem autonomic functions

name: nimcp_arousal_state
observations: Models reticular activating system (RAS)
9 arousal states from COMA to PANIC
Hysteresis prevents oscillation
Rate-limiting for smooth transitions

name: nimcp_circadian
observations: Models suprachiasmatic nucleus (SCN)
8 circadian phases (3 hours each)
Two-process sleep model (Process S + Process C)
Zeitgeber entrainment (light, activity, social)

name: nimcp_protective_cutoff
observations: Models vasovagal/pain withdrawal reflexes
6 protection levels from NORMAL to EMERGENCY_SHUTDOWN
8 threat types with weighted assessment
Capability matrix restricts operations per level

name: nimcp_brainstem_coupling
observations: Models ascending reticular activating system (ARAS)
Bottom-up: arousal, threat, metabolic, circadian signals
Top-down: attention modulation, executive override, voluntary control
Biological latencies: threat 35ms, arousal 75ms, metabolic 1000ms, circadian 30000ms

name: nimcp_medulla_orchestrator
observations: Main coordination module for medulla subsystem
Integrates: health_monitor, recovery, sleep-wake, neuromodulators
Coordination pipeline: circadian -> protection -> arousal -> neuromod -> sleep-wake
Emergency shutdown triggers automatic recovery

name: NIMCP Hippocampus Audit
observations: CORE IMPLEMENTATION STATE: Comprehensive, well-structured system with 23 bridge integrations.
MAIN COMPONENTS: 4 subregions (DG, CA3, CA1, Subiculum), place cells, episodes, ripple buffer
PLACE CELLS: Implemented with Gaussian place fields, context remapping, theta phase precession, associated episodes
GRID CELLS: Mentioned in parahippocampal bridge but not directly implemented in core
PATTERN SEPARATION: DG 1024 cells with 2% sparsity, sparse coding via thresholds implemented
PATTERN COMPLETION: CA3 512 cells with recurrent connections, completion threshold 0.3, attractor dynamics modeled
EPISODIC MEMORY: Full tri-partite encoding (what/where/when) with emotional valence/arousal, 1024 max episodes
CONSOLIDATION: Rates 0.001 per step, sleep/awake replay enabled, consolidation_level tracking (0=hippo, 1=cortex)
MEMORY REPLAY: Sharp-wave ripple buffer (64 events), forward/reverse/compressed modes, sleep consolidation wired
THETA OSCILLATIONS: 8 Hz theta frequency, phase tracking, gamma coupling enabled (40 Hz)
NEUROGENESIS: Enabled for DG cells with maturity tracking (0.0 to 1.0), integration progress for newborns

name: Hippocampus Memory Circuits
observations: TRISYNAPTIC LOOP: DGâ†’CA3â†’CA1 properly implemented with perforant path and Schaffer collaterals
SUBICULUM OUTPUT: Routes to mammillary bodies, entorhinal, prefrontal; boundary cells and grid cells modeled
SPATIAL NAVIGATION: position tracking (3D), place field creation, location-based episode retrieval implemented
BRIDGING: Entorhinal (grid input), Perirhinal (object), Parahippocampal (scene/spatial context), Mammillary (head direction)
NOVEL FEATURES: Prime resonance memory system with tagging and guided retrieval, resonance-enhanced encoding
CONSOLIDATION PATHWAYS: Thalamic relay (anterior nucleus), cortical sync, sleep spindle coupling wired

name: Hippocampus-Imagination Integration
observations: BRIDGE IMPLEMENTATION: hippocampus_imagination_bridge.c fully implemented
EPISODIC FUTURE THINKING: Retrieves past memories (what/where/when), enables constructive replay for imagined futures
PATTERN COMPLETION to IMAGINATION: CA3 pattern completion feeds into imagination engine for scenario generation
PSEUDO-MEMORY ENCODING: Imagination outputs can be encoded as hypothetical episodes for planning
SPATIAL IMAGINATION: Location-based scenario generation via hippocampal place field structure
CONFIGURATION: pattern_completion_weight 0.5, consolidation_boost (imagined memories), replay triggering at 0.7 threshold
LIMITATION: Mostly one-way (hippoâ†’imagination); limited feedback to update memory traces based on imagined outcomes

name: Hippocampus-Language Bridge
observations: BRIDGE IMPLEMENTATION: language_hippocampus_bridge.c fully implemented
WORD MEMORY: Stores 2048 word memories with semantic features, strength, consolidation flag
SEMANTIC MEMORY: Links words to conceptual episodes, semantic associative network (4096 max)
GROUNDING: Word meanings grounded in episodic memories (autobiographical context)
NARRATIVE SUPPORT: Can retrieve memories to construct narratives, link language to spatial-temporal context
CONSOLIDATION CALLBACK: Supports narrative memory consolidation via language module
LIMITATION: Word meanings modeled as feature vectors; limited symbolic/compositional structure for complex semantics

name: Hippocampus Optimization Gaps
observations: GRID CELLS NOT FULLY IMPLEMENTED: Only in parahippocampal bridge; core hippocampus missing Hex coding, scale-free representation
PATTERN COMPLETION THRESHOLD STATIC: Fixed at 0.3; should be adaptive based on memory age, emotional weight, context
NO MEMORY RECONSOLIDATION: Lacks lability mechanismâ€”memories don't re-enter plastic state when retrieved
EPISODIC-SEMANTIC BOUNDARY WEAK: Type field exists but no automatic transition logic from episodicâ†’semantic consolidation
MISSING REVERSE REPLAY: Only forward/compressed modes; reverse replay during sleep is critical for consolidation
NO MEMORY SCULPTING: Lacks competition/interference dynamics; new memories don't degrade similar old ones
PLACE FIELD REMAPPING STUB: has_remapped flag exists but no active context-switching or environmental rotation logic
WEAK INTRINSIC CONNECTIVITY: CA3 recurrence probability fixed at 0.02f; should be learning-dependent
NO PLACE CELL PLASTICITY: Fields don't shrink/expand with experience or modulate with attention/emotion
MEMORY INTERFERENCE NOT MODELED: Cue overlap doesn't trigger interference-based retrieval failures
SPATIAL ENCODING LIMITS: 3D only; no temporal sequence coding or motion trajectory memory
MISSING METACOGNITIVE SIGNALS: No confidence in retrievals, source memory, reality monitoring

name: NIMCP Hypothalamus Audit
observations: DRIVE SYSTEM ARCHITECTURE: 9 drives spanning physiological (hunger, thirst, temperature, fatigue) and psychological (social, curiosity, safety, autonomy, competence)
DRIVE STATES: Level [0,1], urgency/priority, satisfaction, setpoint, deviation, rise_rate, decay_rate, baseline
ALIGNMENT SAFETY: Byrnes steering subsystem integratedâ€”drives as explicit reward function parameters with lock states
ALIGNMENT MODES: CONTROLLED (explicit), SOCIAL_INSTINCT (learned), HYBRID (core controlled, details learned)
HOMEOSTASIS SYSTEM: 10 variables tracked (temperature, glucose, osmolarity, pH, O2, CO2, sleep_pressure, arousal, social, curiosity)
PID CONTROL: Each variable has PI/PD controller with setpoints, Kp/Ki/Kd gains, anti-windup, derivative filtering

NUCLEI MODELED: LH (hunger), VMH (satiety), Anterior/Posterior (thermoregulation), Arcuate (energy balance), PVN (stress/HPA), SCN (circadian), SON (osmolarity), Preoptic (sleep), Tuberomammillary (arousal)
ORCHESTRATOR: Central pub-sub bridge coordinator with 32 bridge slots, event queue, drive aggregation, priority tracking

name: Hypothalamus Drive System Details
observations: CURIOSITY DRIVE: Setpoint 0.6, defined as information-seeking motivation per Gottlieb et al.
COMPETENCE DRIVE: Setpoint 0.6, mastery/skill acquisition motivation
AUTONOMY DRIVE: Setpoint 0.7, self-determination motivation
SAFETY DRIVE: Setpoint 0.8, threat avoidance (highest baseline priority)
SOCIAL DRIVE: Setpoint 0.5, social connection motivation
EXPLORATION WEIGHT: In hippocampus-hypothalamus bridge, exploration_curiosity_weight = 0.3f
EXPLORATION GATING: Curiosity drive has direct FEP bridge (hypo_curiosity_fep_bridge) mapping driveâ†’information gain reduction
DRIVE DECAY: Lower decay_rate when satisfied (0.001 baseline), rise rates increase when unsatisfied

name: Hypothalamus Bridge Network
observations: ATTENTION BRIDGE: Maps drive urgencies to salience category boosts; dominant drive triggers default focus (safety default)
EXECUTIVE BRIDGE: Maps drives to goal priorities; survival drives (safety, hunger) interrupt growth goals (curiosity, competence)
LIMBIC BRIDGE: Receives emotional input (amygdala fear/reward) to modulate stress response
BRAINSTEM BRIDGE: Outputs autonomic signals (sympathetic/parasympathetic) for arousal control
PITUITARY BRIDGE: Neuroendocrine outputs (CRHâ†’ACTHâ†’cortisol for stress)
QUANTUM BRIDGE: Optimization-enabled homeostatic regulation (FEP-style)
HIPPOCAMPUS BRIDGE: 9 memory categories (food, water, shelter, rest, social, threat, exploration, skill, neutral) with encoding priority computation
TRAINING BRIDGE: Modulates learning rate, gradient norm tracking
COGNITIVE HUB BRIDGE: Links to attention/WM/executive control
WORLD MODEL (OMNI) BRIDGE: Prediction-driven resource planning, goal-conditioned prediction
SLEEP BRIDGE: Circadian consolidation signals, sleep pressure homeostasis

name: Hypothalamus-Memory Integration Details
observations: MEMORY ENCODING PRIORITY: baseline 0.2 + encoding_scale * drive_urgency + valence_scale * emotional_strength
EXPLORATION MEMORIES: EXPLORATION category gets prioritized when curiosity drive is high
THREAT MEMORIES: THREAT category gets prioritized when safety drive is high
MEMORY-TO-DRIVE FEEDBACK: Retrieved memories modulate drive levels (food memories increase hunger, threat memories increase safety)
CONSOLIDATION SIGNALS: Circadian phase (2 AM optimal) triggers consolidation during sleep
NAVIGATION GOALS: Exploration drive triggers goal-conditioned navigation via hippocampus
MEMORY DRIVE DECAY: 5% per step, enabling recent memories to transiently boost relevant drives

name: Hypothalamus-Curiosity-Creativity Connection
observations: CURIOSITY DRIVE: 0.6 setpoint, information-seeking motivation
FEP BRIDGE TO CURIOSITY: hypo_curiosity_fep_bridge maps CURIOSITY driveâ†’exploration weight, information gainâ†’FE reduction
IMAGINATION FEP BRIDGE: CURIOSITY drive weights imagination mode (high curiosity = EXPLORATORY mode)
NOVELTY DETECTION: Imagination bridge tracks avg_novelty (scenario novelty [0-1]), feeds back to curiosity drive
SCENARIO GENERATION: High curiosityâ†’exploration modeâ†’diverse scenario generationâ†’novelty assessmentâ†’feedback
CREATIVE POTENTIAL: Pattern completion (CA3) + imagination engine + curiosity drive enables novel idea generation
LIMITATION: CURIOSITYâ†’EXPLORATION is wired but CREATIVITY (combining old ideas in novel ways) not explicitly modeled; lacks transfer learning from memories

name: Hypothalamus Optimization Gaps
observations: DRIVE INTERACTION STATIC: No drive conflict resolution (e.g., hunger vs. social); game theory FEP bridge exists but may not be wired
SETPOINT LEARNING MISSING: Alignment setpoints are hard-coded; no adaptation based on environment/feedback
HOMEOSTASIS OSCILLATIONS: Fixed PID gains (Kp=0.5, Ki=0.1) don't adapt to metabolic state changes
MEMORY CATEGORY REBALANCING: 9 categories fixed; no automatic category emergence based on experience
SLEEP-WAKE PRESSURE WEAK: sleep_pressure variable exists but coupling to consolidation timing not explicit
CIRCADIAN MODULATION LIMITED: SCN clock phase affects consolidation timing but not drive baseline or learning rate
EXPLORATION-EXPLOITATION TRADE-OFF: Curiosity drive pushes exploration but no epsilon-greedy or UCB weighting
STRESS RESPONSE INCOMPLETE: Stressâ†’cortisolâ†’glucose mobilization wired, but immune feedback (cytokines IL-6, TNF) weak
REWARD PREDICTION ERROR: Training bridge tracks gradient but drive learning from RPE not implemented
WORLD MODEL PREDICTION MISMATCH: WM predicts resource availability; drive mismatches not triggering surprise responses
NOVELTY HUNTING UNDERDEVELOPED: Novelty bonuses not integrated with drive system beyond imagination bridge
INTRINSIC MOTIVATION MODELING: Competence/autonomy drives exist but intrinsic motivation reward formulation (self-determined vs. extrinsic) not modeled

name: Hippocampus-Hypothalamus Creative Integration
observations: EPISODIC MEMORYâ†’IMAGINATION: Hippocampus encodes episodes (what/where/when) â†’ imagination reconstructs scenarios (episodic future thinking)
HIPPOCAMPUSâ†’CURIOSITY DRIVE: Recent exploration memories modulate curiosity drive level (active memory keeps drive elevated)
CURIOSITY DRIVEâ†’HIPPOCAMPUS: High curiosity increases encoding priority for EXPLORATION category memories
PATTERN COMPLETIONâ†’CREATIVE RECOMBINATION: CA3 pattern completion can blend multiple memories â†’ novel conceptual combinations
CONSOLIDATION TIMING: Sleep consolidation (hippocampusâ†’cortex) may cross-reference with curiosity drive state, creating curiosity-driven memory schemas
TEMPORAL SEQUENCE MEMORY: Hippocampus codes sequences via temporal context (when) â†’ imagination can chain sequences creatively
EMOTIONAL RESONANCE: Emotional valence/arousal in hippocampus interacts with curiosity drive preference (curious about emotionally salient events)
SPATIAL CONTEXT GROUNDING: Place cells ground abstract concepts spatially â†’ imagination can map ideas to cognitive spaces

name: Hippocampus-Hypothalamus Analytical Integration
observations: MEMORY RETRIEVAL FOR REASONING: Hypothalamus-driven goal states bias hippocampal retrieval (e.g., safety driveâ†’threat memory retrieval)
COMPLETENESS CHECKING: Pattern completion can assess missing information â†’ drives information-seeking curiosity
CONTEXTUAL DECISION-MAKING: Episode-based memory + hippocampal place cells support context-dependent reasoning
MEMORY CONSOLIDATION FOR ABSTRACTION: Sleep consolidation bridges episodicâ†’semantic, enabling rule extraction (analytical learning)
THREAT ASSESSMENT: Threat memories (safety drive) + spatial context (hippocampal place cells) enable risk analysis
COUNTERFACTUAL REASONING: Pattern completion on partial cues + imagination bridge supports 'what if' analysis
CAUSAL INFERENCE: Temporal sequences in memory + hippocampal replay support understanding cause-effect chains
SKILL MEMORY: Procedural episodes + competence drive form basis for skill refinement and transfer

name: Key Integration Points for Enhancement
observations: BIDIRECTIONAL CONSOLIDATION FEEDBACK: Imagination-derived scenarios should influence consolidation priorities when high novelty
CURIOSITY-DRIVEN MEMORY REACTIVATION: Off-line reactivation (sleep) should be biased toward high-curiosity episodes
CREATIVE PRESSURE FROM UNMET GOALS: Incompleteness in pattern completion + goal state should trigger imaginative problem-solving
ANALYTICAL PRESSURE FROM PREDICTION ERROR: World model mismatch + competence drive should trigger systematic memory search for explanations
MEMORY SCHEMA INDUCTION: Repeated consolidation of related episodes should extract schemas (abstract relational structure)
CROSS-DOMAIN ANALOGY: Language grounding + episodic memory + imagination should enable metaphor/analogy generation
NARRATIVE CONSTRUCTION: Language bridge + episode sequences + emotional weighting should construct coherent life narratives
METACOGNITION LINK: Confidence in pattern completion + uncertainty in world model should modulate drive levels (epistemic curiosity)

name: Missing Creative System Connections
observations: NO SEMANTIC SPACE FOR IDEAS: Episodic memory rich but no symbolic semantic workspace for abstract conceptual combination
WEAK CONSTRAINT PROPAGATION: Pattern completion doesn't enforce consistency across multiple retrieved memories
LIMITED MENTAL SIMULATION: Imagination bridge exists but lacks integration with hypothalamic energy/time budgets (can't simulate long sequences)
NO DIVERGENT SEARCH: Curiosity drive doesn't trigger explicit divergent thinking modes (brainstorming with novelty bonuses)
MISSING INSIGHT MECHANISM: No sudden pattern recognition from memory retrieval that triggers aha moment (dopamine reward to curiosity drive)
NO MEMORY INTERFERENCE MODULATION: Similar memories compete for retrieval but drive states don't bias which memory wins
WEAK NARRATIVE CONTINUITY: Language-hippocampus bridge stores word memories but doesn't track narrative coherence over time
NO PLAYFUL VARIATION: Imagination engine doesn't have playful/ludic drive state that enables low-utility exploration

name: Missing Analytical System Connections
observations: NO SYSTEMATIC SEARCH: Hypothalamic goals don't drive systematic memory search (BFS/DFS through memory space)
WEAK CAUSAL INFERENCE: Temporal sequences exist but no causal graphical model or intervention simulation
NO CONSTRAINT SATISFACTION: Analytical reasoning requires finding memories satisfying multiple constraints simultaneously
MISSING EVIDENCE WEIGHTING: Memory retrieval doesn't track reliability/strength for probabilistic reasoning
NO COUNTERFACTUAL SCENARIOS: Pattern completion can fill gaps but doesn't explicitly model counterfactuals (what if different at time T?)
WEAK GOAL PLANNING: Executive bridge has goal priority but lacks memory-based planning (means-ends analysis with episodic knowledge)
NO EXPLANATION GENERATION: Retrieved memories don't automatically generate explanatory narratives for observation mismatches
LIMITED TRANSFER LEARNING: Competence drive triggers skill memory retrieval but doesn't automatically apply lessons to new domains
